overall_summary: The project is approaching a major release with Eliza v3 and Milady integration nearing completion, while concurrently managing a critical technical blocker in the dependency modernization stack. A significant utility milestone was achieved by implementing $ELIZA as the default payment method for x402 infrastructure services.
key_facts: Shaw announced Eliza v3 and Milady integration are nearing completion with a release expected in the coming weeks.
$ELIZA token has been implemented as the default payment method for elizaOS/Milady services in x402 infrastructure.
PR #7146 is identified as a critical blocker for the project's migration to Node.js 24, TypeScript 6, and Bun 1.3.13.
The cloud infrastructure transitioned from Vercel AI Gateway to OpenRouter, using OpenAI and Anthropic as failovers.
Eliza Cloud has commenced operational service for business clients with direct app integration capabilities.
CLI authentication hangs were resolved by implementing auto-clearing for stale Steward and Privy tokens.
A documentation and security vulnerability was identified regarding a non-functional Milady Play Store link in the GitHub repository.
Community coordination remains a priority with the formation of the 'eliza army' steering group and a Hyperscape Discord invite.
open_questions: What is the specific news regarding Milady?
Can we expect some update for $degenai this month?

new_issues_prs.title: PR #7146 (Dependency Blocker)
new_issues_prs.author: unknown
new_issues_prs.significance: Crucial transitive dependency fix required to unblock the entire modernization stack.

new_issues_prs.title: V2.0.0 release
new_issues_prs.author: odilitime
new_issues_prs.significance: Massive modernization of CI/CD and release automation across multiple languages.

new_issues_prs.title: Plugin Proposal: AgentID
new_issues_prs.author: haroldmalikfrimpong-ops
new_issues_prs.significance: Established cryptographic identity and trust layer for agents using Ed25519.

overall_focus.claim: The repository is currently focused on an infrastructure modernization effort targeting Bun 1.3.13 and React 19.2.5, though CI/CD regressions are present due to dependency install failures.

discord_updates.summary: Focus on Eliza v3 readiness and token utility. Discussions shifted from price speculation to infrastructure-linked payments ($ELIZA in x402).
Minimal activity; primarily focused on moderation and removing scammers.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Users expressed significant concern regarding market performance and requested VC partnerships to stabilize token price.
Community members suggested increasing communication frequency and developing clearer PR and business planning materials.

strategic_insights.insight: The project is pivoting toward an indirect token support model—accepting any payment and using revenue for buybacks—to avoid user friction while maintaining buy pressure.
implications_or_questions: Will this buyback model satisfy investors concerned about direct token utility?

strategic_insights.insight: High architectural technical debt in dependencies is causing a deadlock where undetected regressions may leak into the codebase due to failing CI/CD workflows.
implications_or_questions: Is there a manual fallback for PR validation while the Bun install issue persists?

market_analysis.observation: Transition of $ELIZA to a default payment method for x402 infrastructure.
market_analysis.relevance: Moves the token from purely speculative to a service-fee/commodity model.
